post_merge_bugs.description: The sole documented root-README entry point `env -i HOME="$HOME" bash -p spikes/dafny-compat/scripts/run-spike.sh` (run from the repo root) exited 127. The controller cd's into SPIKE_ROOT and relaunches its inner controller via `setsid bash -p -- "$SCRIPT_SELF"`; SCRIPT_SELF held a repo-relative BASH_SOURCE, which no longer resolved from the new cwd, so the inner controller failed to launch.
post_merge_bugs.found_by: external code review of the spike (merged as PR #3); the test suite was green and did not catch it
post_merge_bugs.root_cause: SCRIPT_SELF="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" captured the invocation path verbatim. For the documented invocation this is repo-relative (`spikes/dafny-compat/scripts/run-spike.sh`). The controller later runs `cd -- "$SPIKE_ROOT"` and then `setsid -w bash -p -- "$SCRIPT_SELF"` for the inner-controller relaunch — the relative path is resolved against the new cwd (SPIKE_ROOT) where it does not exist -> exit 127. Only invocations passing an ABSOLUTE path (the test harness via SpikePaths.P, regen-sample.sh via $SCRIPT_DIR) escaped. Fixed by canonicalizing SCRIPT_SELF to $SCRIPT_DIR/basename immediately after SCRIPT_DIR (already absolute) is computed.
post_merge_bugs.why_missed: INV-014 (operator surface: 'the spike is runnable by a stranger') covers exactly this, but its enforcement was specced and implemented as a README keyword-presence check (`Assert.Contains("scripts/run-spike.sh", readme)` in Inv014OperatorSurfaceTests) — a reference check, not an execution (AP-011). Every behavioral test that DOES launch the controller does so through Launch.Script -> ScriptCore, which resolves the script to an ABSOLUTE path via SpikePaths.P and sets WorkingDirectory=SpikeRoot. That launch form is structurally unable to reproduce the documented invocation, which uses a repo-RELATIVE argv[0] from the repo-root cwd — the exact two conditions the bug depends on. The mini-audit added more operator tests (MiniAudit1OperatorTests) but kept the same absolute-path/spike-cwd launch form, so caudit missed it too. The full-regression safety net for the documented entry point was explicitly deferred to the DF-001 clean-checkout (`rm -rf out`) CI job, which is not yet wired. Net: no phase ever executed the exact command the README tells an operator to type.
contributing_causes: spec: INV-014 Enforcement clause specified 'README existence + entry-point reference check' — a doc grep — as the coverage for the documented entry point, so creview/creview-spec/cverify accepted a reference check as 'covered'.

test infrastructure: the only script-launch helper (Launch.ScriptCore) normalizes every invocation to absolute-path + SpikeRoot cwd; a RepoRoot/Repo() helper exists in SpikePaths but no test launches the entry point from the repo root with a relative path.
deferred safety net: DF-001 (the clean-checkout CI job that would exercise the documented entry point directly) is open/unwired.

structural_test_recommended.what: A test that parses each fenced shell command out of spikes/dafny-compat/README.md that invokes a committed script, and executes it VERBATIM from the documented working directory (repo root) with the documented argv[0] form (relative), asserting it gets past the inner-controller relaunch (no exit 127 / 'No such file or directory'). Scope it to a fast smoke of the launch/provision boundary rather than a full ~13-min canonical run.
structural_test_recommended.why: Turns 'the README mentions the script' (AP-011 reference check) and 'a normalized proxy invocation runs' into 'exactly what the operator is told to type, from where they are told to type it, runs'. Catches the whole class: relative-path resolution after cd, cwd assumptions, argv[0] parsing, env stripping.
structural_test_recommended.status: proposed — requires human approval (writes a new test); not applied by this postmortem

spec_enforcement_update_recommended.what: Amend INV-014 Enforcement in .correctless/specs/dafny-compat-spike.md: replace 'README existence + entry-point reference check' with 'a test executes each documented operator command verbatim (documented cwd + relative/absolute argv[0] form as written) and asserts it launches'. A reference/keyword-presence check does not satisfy an operator-surface invariant.
spec_enforcement_update_recommended.status: proposed — requires human approval (edits the approved spec); not applied by this postmortem

post_merge_bugs.description: The full canonical suite is red (1 failure) from any state lacking a prior green suite-receipt in live `out/` — i.e. from a clean checkout (`rm -rf out`). MaIdR22_SuiteReceipt_ProduceSideBinding_AndPathMatchesConstant (MiniAudit2OperatorTests.cs) enumerates prior run roots on disk (out/runid-*, out/regen-*) and asserts at least one PRIOR run's receipts/suite-receipt.json carries suite_exit==0. That green receipt is only produced by a SUCCESSFUL suite, but this test is itself a member of that suite — so the gate can never bootstrap from clean and deadlocks: a failing MaIdR22 makes the suite exit non-zero, so the receipt this run writes records suite_exit != 0, and no subsequent run ever produces the green prior receipt it requires.
post_merge_bugs.found_by: external code review (GPT 5.6) of the spike harness — one of three harness-reliability findings (bug #1); the developer's suite read green only because the working tree carried an accumulated prior green `out/`, masking the deadlock
post_merge_bugs.root_cause: The MA-ID-R2-2 class-fix test validated the PRODUCE side of suite-receipt binding by reading a PRIOR run's receipt discovered on disk (Directory.EnumerateDirectories(out, runid-*/regen-*)) and requiring suite_exit==0, instead of binding to the CURRENT run's receipt produced within this run by the controller/aggregator and surfaced via RunContext. Because the test is itself part of the suite whose success it demands, the required precondition — a green suite-receipt in live out/ — is only producible by a run in which this same test already passed: an unreachable fixpoint from a clean checkout. This regressed the spec's own RS-010 principle (aggregator derives its set from the committed manifest, 'never from reports found on disk'; 'violated when the aggregator iterates reports found on disk') by committing the forbidden disk-iteration / prior-run dependency inside a suite test.
post_merge_bugs.why_missed: The circular gate was INTRODUCED by the mini-audit (round 2) as the class fix for MA-ID-R2-2 (produce-side suite-receipt binding). The mini-audit ran the new test in a working tree that already held an accumulated green `out/` from earlier verification/regen runs, so MaIdR22 passed on leaked prior-run state (AP-010) and the round closed green. cverify (tdd-verify) then marked every invariant 'covered, no weak tests' — but its 'fresh canonical run' (273/273) was launched over that same populated `out/`, not from `rm -rf out`, so it too never exercised the clean-checkout path where the deadlock lives. The authoritative from-clean net was the DF-001 clean-checkout (`rm -rf out`) CI job — the SAME deferred/unwired safety net implicated in PMB-001. No phase ever ran the suite from a state with no accumulated `out/`, which is the only state that reveals the circular gate.
contributing_causes: audit (class-fix quality): QA-022's own rule — 'a class fix is not closed until a test exists that FAILS when the fix is reverted' — was not applied to MaIdR22. It passes or fails on accumulated `out/` state independent of whether the produce-side binding is correct, so it is a wrong-reason test (AP-010) that cannot bootstrap, not a regression guard.

spec: the MA-ID-R2-2 finding framed the produce side around 'the substrate run's receipts/suite-receipt.json' (a prior-run product / out/current referent), baking the prior-run dependency into the corrective action rather than requiring binding to the current run's own receipt.

deferred safety net: DF-001 (from-clean `rm -rf out` CI job) is open/unwired — the same structural gap that let PMB-001 escape now let a second, unrelated post-merge bug escape. Two post-merge bugs share one missing gate.

recurrence: the same 'gate satisfiable only from an already-green / prior state' class already existed as the QA-001+QA-024 evidence-binding deadlock; that earlier instance was dispositioned as an evidence-regeneration caveat (an instance fix), not a class fix — so the class recurred in MaIdR22. Per the constraint on too-narrow prior QA corrective actions, the class fix that should have been applied then is AP-021's from-clean-provability rule plus the current-run-binding enforcement.

structural_gate_recommended.what: Promote DF-001 from deferred to a WIRED executable gate now: run the full canonical suite once from `rm -rf out` (or a fresh clone) and require green, as a local pre-merge gate (`rm -rf out && scripts/run-spike.sh`) until the CI job lands. A from-clean run is the structural detector for the entire AP-021 class — any test depending on accumulated prior-run state fails from clean.
structural_gate_recommended.why: Turns 'the suite is green on my tree' (which silently depended on a leftover green out/) into 'the suite is green from nothing'. One gate catches every prior-run-state dependency at once — including the class that also produced QA-001/QA-024 — and it is the shared missing net behind both PMB-001 and PMB-002, so wiring it discharges risk from two post-merge bugs.
structural_gate_recommended.status: proposed — requires human approval (wires a gate / edits workflow config); not applied by this postmortem

spec_enforcement_update_recommended.what: Amend the MA-ID-R2-2 / suite-receipt enforcement in .correctless/specs/dafny-compat-spike.md: the produce-side binding must be validated against the CURRENT run's receipt — produced within this run by the controller/aggregator and surfaced via RunContext — asserting {run_id, nonce, suite_exit, source_manifest_sha256} against the run-context, provable from `rm -rf out` in a SINGLE run. Reading a prior run's receipt discovered by enumerating `out/runid-*` on disk does NOT satisfy it (it regresses RS-010's 'never from reports found on disk'). Add a general clause: no suite/gate test may require a prior successful run of the same suite as its precondition.
spec_enforcement_update_recommended.status: proposed — requires human approval (edits the approved spec); not applied by this postmortem

corrective_action.instance_fix_note: The instance fix — rewrite MaIdR22 to validate the current run's receipt post-suite via the aggregator/controller and prove convergence from `rm -rf out` — belongs to the bug #1 /ctdd cycle, not this postmortem.

spec.notes: INV-014 operator-surface invariant existed but its Enforcement was specified as a doc-reference check, not an execution — the miss originated in the spec's choice of enforcement level (PMB-001). MA-ID-R2-2 corrective framing bound the produce-side suite-receipt check to 'the substrate run' (a prior-run product / out/current referent) rather than the current run's own receipt, regressing RS-010's never-from-disk principle and admitting a non-bootstrappable circular gate (PMB-002).

tdd-qa.notes: Operator-entry-point invariant 'covered' by a README keyword-presence assert; every controller launch normalized the invocation to absolute-path + fixed cwd, so the documented relative-path/repo-root invocation was never executed (PMB-001). Pattern to watch: entry-point/CLI invariants tested through a normalized proxy launch rather than the documented command form.

tdd-verify.notes: cverify marked INV-014 'covered' on the strength of a reference check + absolute-path launches without flagging that the documented invocation form was never exercised (PMB-001). cverify's 'fresh canonical run' (273/273) was launched over an accumulated `out/`, not from `rm -rf out`, so the clean-checkout deadlock in MaIdR22 was invisible and every invariant was marked 'covered, no weak tests' (PMB-002). Pattern to watch: full-suite-green evidence gathered without a from-clean run — leaked prior-run state hides both entry-point and bootstrap defects.

audit.notes: Mini-audit added operator tests (MiniAudit1OperatorTests) but reused the same absolute-path/spike-cwd launch helper, so the form-specific defect escaped audit too (PMB-001). Worse, the mini-audit INTRODUCED a circular/bootstrap gate (MaIdR22) as the class fix for MA-ID-R2-2 and closed the round green on leaked prior-run `out/` state (AP-010); QA-022's own 'a class fix is not closed until a test FAILS on revert' rule was not applied to it (PMB-002). Pattern to watch: audit-authored class-fix tests not proven from a clean (`rm -rf out`) state.
